ead.archdesc:
======= RMA00073Dwight Sanderson papers21-33-73Compiled by A. LipskyDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
February 20032B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
cals socscThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:20:24 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Dwight Sanderson papers
Sanderson, Dwight, 1878-1944.
21-33-73
7.8 cubic feet. 7.8 cubic feet.
1894-1960,-1894-1943 (bulk) Diaries, bibliographies, manuscripts, student papers, correspondence, publications, a scrapbook, photograph albums, and class materials. Includes 43 annual diaries of appointments; bibliographies; student papers; correspondence; papers on the family, marriage, and other topics; lecture outlines for classes on rural sociology, social psychology, and sociology; publications by Sanderson; scrapbook of articles following his retirement; post retirement letters; a photocopied excerpt from A Brief History of Entomology at the Agricultural and Mechanical College of Texas concerning Sanderson and Glenn W. Herrick (1960); five albums of photographs and postcards from European travels, 1925/1926 and 1935; and other materials pertaining to Sanderson's work in rural sociology and related fields. Also, letter from Pearl Buck, written from Nanking, China, July 6, 1930. Collection material in English 2082777
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
A. Lipsky
Date completed:
October 1983
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, February 2003
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, October 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of rural sociology, New York State College of Agriculture at Cornell University.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes 43 annual diaries of appointments; bibliographies; student papers; correspondence; papers on the family, marriage, and other topics; lecture outlines for classes on rural sociology, social psychology, and sociology; publications by Sanderson; scrapbook of articles following his retirement; post retirement letters; a photocopied excerpt from A Brief History of Entomology at the Agricultural and Mechanical College of Texas concerning Sanderson and Glenn W. Herrick (1960); five albums of photographs and postcards from European travels, 1925/1926 and 1935; and other materials pertaining to Sanderson's work in rural sociology and related fields. Also, letter from Pearl Buck, written from Nanking, China, July 6, 1930.
Cite As:
Dwight Sanderson papers, #21-33-73. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scrapbooks. Diaries. Bibliographies. Europe -- Description and travel. Sociologists. College teachers. Social psychology. Sociology, Rural. Sociology. Families. Herrick, Glenn W. (Glenn Washington), 1870-1965. Buck, Pearl S. (Pearl Sydenstricker), 1892-1973. New York State College of Agriculture. Department of Rural Sociology Cornell University -- : Faculty.
2082777 <<<<<<< HEAD Four Years of County Planning & Development (mimeographed copy)Sept. 19381A Demonstration in County Development (3 bound copies)19351Tompkins County Schools (3 folders)1922-19391The Family2
Scope and Contents
(All of the following are from Chapters I, VIII, XI, XII, XIII, XIV; Chapters XV, XVI, XVII, XXI were noted in collection as missing)Physiology and Psychology of Petting, Legal Aspects of Betrothal19322The Prediction of Adjustment in Marriage19322Husband-Wife Relations in Marriage19322Family Bibliography19322Extracts from Thomas-Tusser's Book on Huswifery" (edition of 1580).19322Federal Extension Service (Boy's and Girl's Club Work)19182FERA (Federal Emergency Relief Admin. )19342Group I & II1937-19432Rural Organization 16 Lecture Outlines, Notes1918-19213Rural Organization Miscellanea3Rural Secondary Schools, Social Studies1915-3Rural Sociology I-IV1939-19433Social Psychology3Sociology 15 (Introductory)19153"State Departments of Agriculture " by Dwight Sanderson. Written at University of Chicago in 1916.19163Annual Cornell School for Missionaries1930-19404"Moral Education in the Public Schools"4Philosophy 31 (Paper presented at University of Chicago)Dec. 16, 19164Psychology (Abnormal) Paper,, and Sociology Paper prepared by D. Sanders on at University of Chicago19154"A Psychological Account of the Program of Religion" - Paper given at University of ChicagoNov. 2, 19174Rural Community I -VI I I1917-19414Rural Community, No. VIII-XI1925-19445"Rural Community Organization for Religious Education" -Rural Church Seminar19175Problems of Rural Democracy19405Rural Organization, No. l-III1917-19245Memorandum concerning Proposed objects of the American Farm Bureau Federation (Prepared and circulated privately at the time of the first meeting of the American Farm Bureau Federation in Chicago. )19206Rural Organization - IV-IX1917-19226Association Press (National Council of the YMCA)1939-19417County Agents CourseMainly 19317Depression Problems, I-II (much correspondence in preparation of Sanderson's manuscript on "New Problems of Rural Youth )1936-19377The Family. Chapters I through VIII. Chapters 14, 15, 16, 17 & 21 are missing.7Factual Material for the Discussion of "Trends in American Standards of Living" by L.S. Cottrell19377The Group III1928-19348Leadership I-IV (Farm Bureau, Scrapbook, Leadership, Letters & Bibliographies, student papers . Includes a mimeo copy of "Rural Leadership" by D. Sanderson1917-19418Miscellaneous correspondence re E.D. Sanderson. Also, various awards and articles.1901-19439Miscellaneous Pictures (mostly negatives) and Postcards.192510Diaries; correspondence concerning publication of Sanderson's works, such as "The Farmer and his Community," "Elementary Entomology", "Insect Pests of Farm, Garden and Orchard", and job opportunities around the country.1899-195011All illustrations and photographs used in books by D. Sandersonn.d.12Entomological Publications1898-190313New Hampshire Entomology1904-191413"Report of the West Virginia Agricultural Experiment station"1913-191413The Rural Community193213Rural Sociology and Rural Social Organization194213All the above are by D. Sanderson13Texas Entomology1902-190614"Report of the West Virginia Agricultural Experiment Station"1911-191214"Report of the State Board of Regents, West Virginia"1911-191214First Biennial Report, State Crop Pest Commission, West Virginia1913-14Report of the New York State Food Supply Commission191814The American Journal of Sociology, pp. 117-260Nov. 194414Rural Sociology, Vol. II, No. 1Mar. 194614Volume Report: "The Contribution of Extension Methods, and Techniques Toward the Rehabilitation of War Torn Countries"194514Scrapbook of early works1894-190214Scrapbook of early themes and copies of the Hesperian Star from the Agricultural College in Michigan.1893-189614NYS Food Supply Commission. Preliminary Report of the Census of the Agricultural Resources of New York State. Bulletin No. 2191714Bulletin of the NYS Food Supply Commission. Report of the Conference.191714Articles, letters re retirement, and letters of condolence to the Sanderson Family, including a black binder of newspaper obituaries. Also two reprints: A Biography of Ezra Dwight Sanderson by W.A. Anderson, and a bibliography of Sanderson up to Sept. 25, 1943.194315Brief biographical sketches of Sanderson and Glenn W. Herrick, publ. 1960, 8 pp. photocopy.196015Letter from Pearl S. Buck.July 6, 193015"The Family in the Life of Today", Handbook for leaders of discussion groups.n.d.16"With Love and Affection, Respectfully Yours, Graduate Students". (A bound collection of laudatory letters from graduate students to D. Sanderson upon his retirement.194316One bound volume of early work (including high school themes) by D. Sanderson.1893-189716One scrapbook of school material and grades through college; including various souvenir programs and invitations.1885-190116One student notebook on Entomology with hand drawn insect examples.1930-193316One scrapbook of insect photographs and newspaper clippings.191016One scrapbook of newspaper clippings re: the Tompkins County Development Association.1934-193516Two folders: Contents of a scrapbook of clippings and printed items.1903-190716Album containing postcards and photographs1925-1926171
Scope and Contents
Locations include France and one in Germany, including Amance, Amiens, Avignon, Bar-le-Duc, Bazancourt, Bellefosse, Bormes, Buire-sur-L'Ancre, Chalons-sur-Marne, Chanteheux, Courtisols, Einville, Fouday, Fressin, Le Treport, Giens, Ribaud, Givry-en-Argonne, Hangest-en-Santerre, Hyeres, La Ferte-Chevresis, Laon, Le Havre, Lyon, Metz, Motteville, Nancy, Paris, Reims, Ribemont, Rilly-la-Montagne, Rouen, Rouffach, Salins-des-Pesquiers, Seichamps, Sezanne, Solbach, Strasbourg, Tronville-en-Barrois, Vertus, Vitry-le-Francois, Witry-les-Reims, and Schwarzwald.Album containing postcards and photographs1925-1926172
Scope and Contents
Locations include France, including Aix-en-Provence, Alps, Arles, Auray, Barjoles, Bordeaux, Camaret, Carnac, Chateaulin, Cite de Carcassone, Correns, Crozon, Damazan, Draguignan, Gardanne, Grimaldi, Guillaumes, L'Aveyron, La Feuillee, La Haute-Garrone, La Rochelle, Landivisiau, Marseille, Menton, Merville, Monaco, Monte-Carlo, Montmeyan, Montpellier, Morlaix, Nerac, Nice, Nimes, Puch, Quimper, Quinson, Rennes, Roscoff, Saint-Malo, Saint-Pol-de-Leon, Sanremo, Sospel, Tonneins, Toulouse, Vianne, and Villefranche.Album containing postcards and photographs1925-1926183
Scope and Contents
Locations include France and Italy, including Amboise, Bernay, Blois, Chambord, Chemonceaux, Courtonne-la-Meurdrac, Grand-Champs, La Bretagne, Laval, Le Mans, Lisieux, Loches, Locmariaquer, Marolles, Sologne, Thiberville, Tours, Vannes, and Florence, Genoa, Milan, Rome, and Venice.Album containing postcards and photographs1925-1926184
Scope and Contents
Locations include Switzerland, Germany, the Czech Republic, Poland, Denmark, the Netherlands, Belgium, including Bern, Lucerne, Romanshorn, Zurich, Berlin, Dresden, Leipzig, Munich, Potsdam, Cesky Brod, Olomouc, Warsaw, Liskow, Aaskov, Copenhagen, Frederiksborg, Haraldsborg, Kronborg, Odense, Roskilde, Svogerslev, Vejen, Amsterdam, Groningen, Volendam, Wageningen, Winschoten, Brussels, and Leuven.Album containing postcards and photographs1925-1926195
Scope and Contents
Locations include Belgium, England, Scotland, France, Italy, Germany, and the Netherlands, including Diksmuide, Ypres, Bruges, Brussels, London, Warwik, Oxford, Grasmere, Durham, Lincoln, Ely, Cambridge, Alloway, Trossachs, Glasgow, Edinburgh, Paris, Tours, Rome, Milan, Berlin, and Amsterdam.Album containing postcards and photographs1935206
Scope and Contents
Locations include Italy, Croatia, Serbia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Greece, Bulgaria, Romania, including Trieste, Gradiste, Mraclin, Nova Gradiska, Tomasica, Zagreb, Kovacevac, Orasje, Veria,Lovech, Cismigiu, and Bucharest.Album containing postcards and photograph1935207
Scope and Contents
Locations include Ireland, Scotland, Wales, England, France, Luxembourg, the former Yugoslavia, Monaco, Switzerland, Germany, and Belgium, including Cashel, Connemara, Foxford, Galway, Kilkenny, Killarney, Larne, Limerick, Londonderry, Sligo, Tipperary, Abbottsford, Aberfeldy, Alloway, Dunfermline, Edinburgh, Fortingall, Glen Coe, Inverness, Loch Lomond, Aberystwyth, Carnarvon, Canterbury, Kenilworth, Aubenas, Blois, Briancon, Eze, Nimes, Paris, Puget-Théniers, Rueil-Malmaison, Sion, Versailles, Diekirch, Brig, Gletsch, Lucerne, Bingen, Heidelberg, Herrenalb, Murg, Offenburg, Worms, and Brussels.Diplomas from the University of Michigan, BS 1897; Cornell University, BS 1898; University of Chicago, Ph.D 1921; and certificates from Phi Kappa Phi, the Department of State for the 12th Congress of the International Institute of Sociology, Ministry of Agriculture of Belgium, and the Country of Luxembourg1898-19351 ======= Four Years of County Planning & Development (mimeographed copy)Sept. 19381A Demonstration in County Development (3 bound copies)19351Tompkins County Schools (3 folders)1922-19391The Family2
Scope and Contents
(All of the following are from Chapters I, VIII, XI, XII, XIII, XIV; Chapters XV, XVI, XVII, XXI were noted in collection as missing)Physiology and Psychology of Petting, Legal Aspects of Betrothal19322The Prediction of Adjustment in Marriage19322Husband-Wife Relations in Marriage19322Family Bibliography19322Extracts from Thomas-Tusser's Book on Huswifery" (edition of 1580).19322Federal Extension Service (Boy's and Girl's Club Work)19182FERA (Federal Emergency Relief Admin. )19342Group I & II1937-19432Rural Organization 16 Lecture Outlines, Notes1918-19213Rural Organization Miscellanea3Rural Secondary Schools, Social Studies1915-3Rural Sociology I-IV1939-19433Social Psychology3Sociology 15 (Introductory)19153"State Departments of Agriculture " by Dwight Sanderson. Written at University of Chicago in 1916.19163Annual Cornell School for Missionaries1930-19404"Moral Education in the Public Schools"4Philosophy 31 (Paper presented at University of Chicago)Dec. 16, 19164Psychology (Abnormal) Paper,, and Sociology Paper prepared by D. Sanders on at University of Chicago19154"A Psychological Account of the Program of Religion" - Paper given at University of ChicagoNov. 2, 19174Rural Community I -VI I I1917-19414Rural Community, No. VIII-XI1925-19445"Rural Community Organization for Religious Education" -Rural Church Seminar19175Problems of Rural Democracy19405Rural Organization, No. l-III1917-19245Memorandum concerning Proposed objects of the American Farm Bureau Federation (Prepared and circulated privately at the time of the first meeting of the American Farm Bureau Federation in Chicago. )19206Rural Organization - IV-IX1917-19226Association Press (National Council of the YMCA)1939-19417County Agents CourseMainly 19317Depression Problems, I-II (much correspondence in preparation of Sanderson's manuscript on "New Problems of Rural Youth )1936-19377The Family. Chapters I through VIII. Chapters 14, 15, 16, 17 & 21 are missing.7Factual Material for the Discussion of "Trends in American Standards of Living" by L.S. Cottrell19377The Group III1928-19348Leadership I-IV (Farm Bureau, Scrapbook, Leadership, Letters & Bibliographies, student papers . Includes a mimeo copy of "Rural Leadership" by D. Sanderson1917-19418Miscellaneous correspondence re E.D. Sanderson. Scope and Contents
Locations include France and one in Germany, including Amance, Amiens, Avignon, Bar-le-Duc, Bazancourt, Bellefosse, Bormes, Buire-sur-L'Ancre, Chalons-sur-Marne, Chanteheux, Courtisols, Einville, Fouday, Fressin, Le Treport, Giens, Ribaud, Givry-en-Argonne, Hangest-en-Santerre, Hyeres, La Ferte-Chevresis, Laon, Le Havre, Lyon, Metz, Motteville, Nancy, Paris, Reims, Ribemont, Rilly-la-Montagne, Rouen, Rouffach, Salins-des-Pesquiers, Seichamps, Sezanne, Solbach, Strasbourg, Tronville-en-Barrois, Vertus, Vitry-le-Francois, Witry-les-Reims, and Schwarzwald.Album containing postcards and photographs1925-1926172
